ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2010 Continued To Nov-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox for Paul McCalope Sr.(Deceased) Adminstrator of the Estate of Mary Christopher (Beneficiary of Josephine) . Kevin Ford for Barbara Cole. Elijah Keys for Olivia Pollard Epps (Conservator of Debbie Ellis/Beneficiary). Paul McCalope Jr. (Son of Paul Sr/Nephew of Decedent) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Fox stated that Paul McCalope Sr. passed away in August, that they were trying to figure out who would pick up administration of the two estates (one in Florida/one in San Francisco), that the Petition for Appointment of Successsor Administrator by Paul Jr. for the estate here would be filed tomorrow and that all Parties would be communicating to try to figure out how the best way to proceed. The matter was continued to November 17, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1740 Quint Street, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar May-13-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Ford for and with Barbara Perkins Cole (Administrator).

Mr. Ford provided an endorsed copy of the re-appraisal filed on May 11, 2009 appraising the property on May 5, 2009 at $435,000.00. The buyer was present in Court. The sale was confirmed at $430,000.00. The proceeds are to be deposited into a blocked account. Mr. Ford will file the judicial counsel form for proof of the blocked account. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR#7077. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-26-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Kevin Ford appearing for and with Barbara Perkins Cole. Paul McCalope, objector is in court. Mr. McCalope has sent a letter to counsel with copy to the court asking for a continuance. He is objecting to the petition because he has been living in the property and he has been taking care of business. Court is appointing Ms. Perkins Cole as the administrator with will annexed with limited authority. Sale will be subject to court confirmation. Bond is fixed in the amount of $10,000.00. Order signed. Ex parte order dispensing with notice also signed. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
